98.reason: Queue indices are initialized to -1 and -2, causing out-of-bounds array access in enq/deq. They should be initialized to 0 and -1 respectively. Additionally, these global variables are not reset between BFS traversals, leading to incorrect queue behavior on subsequent calls.

reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-1: 0.1111111111111111 , reason:  queue indices should be initialized to 0 and -1 respectively to avoid out-of-bounds access.
reasons.buggy-deepseek-reasoner-2: 0.16666666666666666 , reason:  Incorrect initialization. Should be start=0, end=-1 for an empty queue. Using negative indices leads to out-of-bounds access.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-1: 0.2 , reason:  These global queue indices are not reset before each BFS traversal in `travelbfs`, which will cause incorrect queue behavior and output on subsequent BFS calls.
reasons.buggy-qwen3.6-plus-2: 0.25 , reason:  Queue indices are initialized to -1 and -2, causing out-of-bounds array access in enq/deq. They should be initialized to 0 and -1 respectively.
